summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this class, `CompressedSplitLineReader`, is to extend the functionality of `SplitLineReader` to handle reading records from compressed splits correctly, avoiding data loss or duplication.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic revolves around the `fillBuffer` method, which is overridden to handle reading from a `SplitCompressionInputStream`. It checks if the split ended in the middle of a record or delimiter and decides whether to read an additional record to prevent data loss or duplication. The `readLine` method is also overridden to limit reading to one additional record after the split has ended.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- INPUTS: The class takes a `SplitCompressionInputStream`, a `Configuration` object, and a byte array `recordDelimiterBytes` as inputs.
- OUTPUTS: It reads lines of text from a compressed split, handling edge cases where the split ends in the middle of a record or delimiter.

DEPENDENCIES:
- `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.input.SplitLineReader`
- `java.io.IOException`
- `java.io.InputStream`
- `org.apache.hadoop.io.Text`
- `org.apache.hadoop.io.compress.SplitCompressionInputStream`
- `org.apache.hadoop.conf.Configuration`

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: The main purpose of this class is to extend the functionality of the SplitLineReader for uncompressed files, ensuring correct splitting even with multi-byte delimiters. It also handles reading an additional record if the split ends in the middle of a record delimiter.

CORE LOGIC: The core logic involves overriding methods from the parent class SplitLineReader to manage reading from an uncompressed file input stream. The `fillBuffer` method determines how many bytes to read from the input stream based on the remaining length of the current split. If the split ends in the middle of a record delimiter, it sets a flag to read an additional record in the next read operation.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS: The class takes a `FSDataInputStream` (an input stream for reading HDFS files), a `Configuration` object (for Hadoop configuration), a byte array `recordDelimiterBytes` (to define the record delimiter), and a `long splitLength` (the length of the current split to read). It outputs `Text` objects, which represent lines read from the input stream.

DEPENDENCIES: The class depends on several Hadoop core classes:
- `org.apache.hadoop.classification`: For annotations like `@InterfaceAudience` and `@InterfaceStability`.
- `org.apache.hadoop.conf`: For the `Configuration` class.
- `org.apache.hadoop.fs`: For `FSDataInputStream`.
- `org.apache.hadoop.io`: For the `Text` class.
- `org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.input`: For the parent class `SplitLineReader`.
